phenomenon: Fairness principle selection under budget constraints
claim: Dictators apply different weights to justice principles depending on whether they earned resources and what reciprocity signals the recipient offers.

phenomenon: Ordinary concept of weakness of will
claim: Weakness of will is best understood as a prototype concept with multiple overlapping features, not a logically defined category with necessary and sufficient conditions.

phenomenon: Parafoveal processing and word-frequency load in reading
constructs: preview benefit foveal load perceptual span frequency
claim: The size of the parafoveal processing window and the amount of preview benefit from distant words depend on the cognitive load imposed by intervening words.

phenomenon: AIDS pandemic effects on non-AIDS health services
constructs: health-system capacity resource allocation disease burden
claim: Regions bearing heavy AIDS burdens experienced deterioration in antenatal care, trained delivery attendance, and child immunizations, starting in the mid-1990s.

Partnership has symmetric effects on well-being for both formation and dissolution, with similar magnitudes across same-sex and different-sex couples, but gender-specific patterns emerge for same-sex partnerships.

phenomenon: Moral judgment of omissions versus commissions
claim: When intentions and outcomes are equivalent, people judge harmful commissions as more immoral than harmful omissions, partially because they attribute greater causal role to commissions.
